action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 23 (logit averaging TTA) scored 0.2489 AP, below the current best of 0.2533 from iteration 21 (probability averaging, 32 TTA runs per model x 2 models). Only ~12 minutes remain, so no training is possible. Analysis: Probability averaging works better than logit averaging for this model/task. A novel, zero-cost inference improvement not yet tried is weight-space averaging (model soup) between the best validation checkpoint and its EMA counterpart, followed by TTA. Because the model uses LayerNorm (no running statistics), weight averaging is safe and can produce a model that generalizes better than prediction ensembling. I will create a 50/50 weight soup from the two state dicts, run 64 stochastic forward passes on the soup (equivalent total compute to the best prior eval), average probabilities, and submit eval. This is the last high-ROI experiment possible in the remaining time.
